artifactDemos.title: Create a Virtual Xylophone
artifactDemos.value: Create a HTML-based xylophone with colorful keys within an {{ARTIFACT}}. Provide visual feedback for struck keys, and generate simple, realistic sounds using the Web Audio API based on frequency parameters. Include basic instructions.

artifactDemos.title: Create a Food-Eating Emoji Game
artifactDemos.value: Create a simple HTML-based game, similar to Catch the Candy, within an {{ARTIFACT}}. The player controls an emoji at the bottom of the screen, moving it rapidly left and right to catch and eat food items (e.g., 🍓, 🍔, 🍎) falling from above while avoiding bombs (💣).Each time the player catches a food emoji, their emoji will change to a more exaggerated positive expression (e.g., 🤩, 😆, 😋) to indicate success. If the player accidentally catches a bomb, the emoji will first show an explosion (💥) and then change to a dizzy expression (😵‍💫).

addSummaryButton.prompt: Detect whether the current page is long-form content (article, blog post, documentation, etc.) using content length and structural heuristics. If yes, inject a "Summary" button floating in the top-right. When clicked, generate a summary and render it as HTML in the page's reading format (not raw markdown) inside a slide-out right panel.
addSummaryButton.title: Add Summary Button

autoScrollPage.prompt: Add smooth auto-scrolling to this page so I can read hands-free.
autoScrollPage.title: Auto-scroll page

changeColorScheme.prompt: Apply a warm color scheme to this page for a more comfortable look.

explainThisTerm.prompt: On any SaaS dashboard, use AI to detect navigation items, labels, and menu entries containing domain-specific jargon, acronyms, or product-specific terms. Exclude common UI words such as Settings, Dashboard, Export, Save, Profile. When the user hovers over a flagged term for ~800ms, display a small "Explain This" button near the term. Clicking it opens a tooltip with a plain-language explanation generated by AI. The button must not block normal interactions.
explainThisTerm.title: Explain This Term

hideSidebar.prompt: Hide the sidebar and navigation panels to focus on the main content.
hideSidebar.title: Hide sidebar

improveReadability.prompt: Improve this page readability with better spacing, typography, and visual hierarchy.
improveReadability.title: Improve readability

increaseFontSize.prompt: Increase the font size across this page for easier reading.
increaseFontSize.title: Increase font size

inlineTranslator.prompt: On long-form content pages only, inject a "Translate" button in the top-right. Support major languages: English, Chinese, Japanese, Spanish, French, German, Korean, Arabic, Portuguese, Russian. Offer three view modes: translation only, original only, side-by-side. Use the plugin's built-in AI API to translate, and render the translated text directly within the article flow.
inlineTranslator.title: Inline Translator

removeAds.prompt: Remove ads and distracting promo blocks from this page while keeping the main layout.
removeAds.title: Remove Ads

themeSwitcher.prompt: On long-form content pages only (detect by content length and structure), inject a theme switcher button in the top-right. Offer at least 4 themes: Light, Dark, Sepia, High-contrast. Persist the user's choice in localStorage so it survives page reloads.
themeSwitcher.title: Theme Switcher

amazonRecommendBestProduct.prompt: On search result pages, add a "Find Best" button above the results. When clicked, analyze the top visible products using price, star rating, review count, and computed unit price, then recommend the single best option in a floating chat panel with explicit reasoning.
amazonRecommendBestProduct.title: Recommend Best Product

amazonRemoveSponsoredProducts.prompt: Remove all Sponsored Products and Sponsored Brands units across search result pages, product detail pages, and category/brand listing pages. Do not hide organic results.
amazonRemoveSponsoredProducts.title: Remove Sponsored Products

amazonShowPriceHistoryLink.prompt: On the product detail page, inject a "Price History" button next to the main price. Clicking it opens CamelCamelCamel for the current ASIN in a new tab. Do not attempt to render a price history chart inline unless an official data source is available.
amazonShowPriceHistoryLink.title: Show Price History Link

amazonShowUnitPrice.prompt: For any product where a precise unit price can be derived (e.g., per fish-oil capsule, per tissue, per fl oz, per sheet), calculate the unit price and display it with a colored badge next to the main price, on both search result cards and the product detail page.
amazonShowUnitPrice.title: Show Unit Price

ar5ivExplainFigureOrTable.prompt: On arXiv paper HTML pages (ar5iv), inject an "Explain this" button next to every figure and table. On click, AI analyzes the figure/table together with its caption and surrounding text to produce: strongest results, anomalies, ablation conclusions, possible overclaims. Render the explanation as an overlay on the figure with arrow annotations pointing to specific regions.
ar5ivExplainFigureOrTable.title: Explain Figure Or Table

ar5ivExplainForBeginner.prompt: On arXiv HTML pages (ar5iv), when the user selects a paragraph and holds the selection for ~500ms, show an "Explain for Beginner" button near the selection. On click, generate a plain-language explanation with an analogy and a step-by-step breakdown, displayed in a tooltip near the selection.
ar5ivExplainForBeginner.title: Explain For Beginner

arxivCitationKnowledgeGraph.prompt: On an arXiv paper page, add a "Map Citations" button in the bottom-right corner. When clicked, use the Semantic Scholar API (lookup by the arXiv ID extracted from the URL) to fetch the paper's references and citing papers. Generate a knowledge graph with the current paper as the central node, surrounded by related papers color-coded by relationship type: foundation, compare, improve, critique. On hover show "why related"; on click open that paper. Support zoom in and out.
arxivCitationKnowledgeGraph.title: Citation Knowledge Graph

arxivPaperContributionMap.prompt: On an arXiv paper page, add a "What's new?" button. When clicked, fetch the full paper text from the ar5iv HTML version (https://ar5iv.org/abs/<arxivId>) and extract Problem / Prior Work / Novelty / Evidence / Limitations. Render the result as an infographic card in a side panel, and highlight the source paragraphs in the original paper.
arxivPaperContributionMap.title: Paper Contribution Map

chatgptAddRebuttalButton.prompt: Below each assistant response, add a "Rebuttal" button with no icon, matching the existing UI style. When clicked, use AI to identify the weakest or most controversial claim in that response, and populate the input field with a natural-sounding, first-person follow-up that challenges it—ready to send without any editing.
chatgptAddRebuttalButton.title: Add Rebuttal Button

chatgptCategorizeChatHistory.prompt: In the left sidebar, use AI to auto-classify each conversation by topic (e.g., coding, writing, research, misc) and group them into collapsible categories. Persist categories to localStorage so they survive reloads. Add a search box at the top of the sidebar.
chatgptCategorizeChatHistory.title: Categorize Chat History

chatgptPinConversations.prompt: Add a pin icon next to each conversation in the left sidebar. Clicking it pins the conversation to a dedicated "Pinned" section at the top of the sidebar; clicking again unpins. Persist pinned state in localStorage so it survives reloads.

googleAdsWhyIsThisDropping.prompt: On every page of the Google Ads interface, inject a "Why is this dropping?" button in the top toolbar. When clicked, analyze the currently visible page data (extracted from the DOM) and produce: (1) underperforming or abnormal metrics with likely causes and actionable optimization suggestions; (2) well-performing metrics with reasons behind the success. Render the output as markdown in a floating panel anchored to the bottom-right. The panel is collapsible via the same button and reopens on re-click. Include a "Re-analyze" button inside the panel. When the user navigates to a sub-page and clicks Re-analyze, detect the new page context and re-extract data without requiring a full page refresh—never return "no data available" on a valid page.
googleAdsWhyIsThisDropping.title: Why Is This Dropping

googleCalendarAddSummaryButton.prompt: Inject a "Summary" button into the top toolbar. When clicked, summarize today's schedule into a floating right-side panel with three sections: chronological event list (gaps flagged), overlaps or back-to-backs to watch out for, and suggested prep/travel buffers.
googleCalendarAddSummaryButton.title: Add Summary Button

googleCleanFirstScreen.prompt: On the search results page, keep only the search input box, organic result items, and pagination. Remove everything else, including People also ask, knowledge panel, image/video carousels, discussions and forums, related searches, and any third-party injected widgets.

googleDocsAddAiSummaryButton.prompt: Add an "AI Summary" button to the top-right of the toolbar. When clicked, summarize the entire document and display the summary in a slide-out right panel with a copy-to-clipboard button.

googleDocsDarkMode.prompt: Transform the Google Docs interface into an immersive dark mode, covering everything: the top menu bar, toolbar, ruler, page canvas background, and any side panels. Keep paragraph text fully legible on the dark page (invert chrome, not content readability). After applying, take a screenshot to verify nothing was missed.

googleDocsFocusWritingMode.prompt: Turn Google Docs into a focused writing mode: hide the title bar, menu bar, toolbar, ruler, and all side panels, leaving only the document canvas centered on screen. Add a small toggle button in the top-right to restore the normal view.
googleDocsFocusWritingMode.title: Focus Writing Mode

googleHideAiOverview.prompt: Remove the AI Overview block from search results and show organic results starting from the top of the page.

googleHighlightAds.prompt: Highlight all ads (including Sponsored results and Shopping carousels) with a visible colored border. Strictly target only the ad containers themselves; do not highlight organic results.
googleHighlightAds.title: Highlight Ads

googleMapsExportResultsToCsv.prompt: On a Maps search results page, extract all visible business cards from the results panel (name, address, star rating, review count, phone, website if shown). Paginate through as many results as possible by scrolling the results list. Inject an "Export CSV" button at the top of the results panel that downloads the collected list as a CSV file.
googleMapsExportResultsToCsv.title: Export Results To CSV

googleRemoveRelatedSuggestions.prompt: Remove the "People also ask", "People also search for", and "See results about" blocks from the search results page.
googleRemoveRelatedSuggestions.title: Remove Related Suggestions

googleRemoveSponsoredAds.prompt: Remove all advertisements on the search results page, including Sponsored results at top/bottom and Shopping ad carousels.
googleRemoveSponsoredAds.title: Remove Sponsored Ads

googleScholarBuildLitReviewMatrix.prompt: On Google Scholar search results, inject a checkbox on each result card. When the user selects ≥2 papers, show a floating "Build Lit Review Matrix" button. On click, use AI to extract method / dataset / findings / limitations / gap for each selected paper from its title, snippet, and abstract where available. Mark each field's confidence as "low" when inferred from snippet only. Render the output as a matrix table with a clustering view.
googleScholarBuildLitReviewMatrix.title: Build Lit Review Matrix

googleScholarTagPaperTypes.prompt: On Google Scholar search results, use AI to tag each result card with one of: seminal, survey, benchmark, beginner-friendly, risky. Display the tag as a colored badge plus an explicit confidence level (e.g., "likely survey (low confidence)"). Tags are inferred from title and snippet only, so low-confidence results must be explicitly labeled as such.
googleScholarTagPaperTypes.title: Tag Paper Types

googleSheetsAddAiRangeSummary.prompt: Add an "AI Summary" button floating in the bottom-right corner of the page (must not overlap the toolbar). When clicked, let the user drag-select a range; then generate a summary of the selected range's content and display it in a floating panel anchored to the bottom-right.
googleSheetsAddAiRangeSummary.title: Add AI Range Summary

googleSheetsDarkMode.prompt: > On the current Google Sheets page, inject a floating dark mode toggle button and implement a dark mode with the following exact specifications: > > **Button requirements:** > - Position the button in the empty whitespace to the right of the document title (e.g., "Untitled spreadsheet"), in the top header bar — NOT overlapping the menu bar ("File / Edit / View…") below it. > - Use `position: fixed` with roughly `top: 8px; left: 290px`, height around 24px, padding `3px 10px`, font-size 12px, rounded pill shape (`border-radius: 16px`), and a very high `z-index` (e.g., `2147483647`) so it sits above Google's UI layers. > - Default state: blue background (`#1a73e8`), white text, label `"🌙 Dark Mode"`. > - Active state (when dark mode is on): label changes to `"☀️ Light Mode"`. > - Give it a unique id like `dark-mode-toggle-btn` and remove any previous instance before re-injecting (idempotent). > > **Dark mode implementation (critical — use CSS filter inversion, NOT manual color overrides):** > - Inject a `<style>` tag with id `dark-mode-toggle-style`. > - When active, add a class (e.g., `dm-on`) to `<html>` that applies `filter: invert(1) hue-rotate(180deg); background: #ffffff;` to the entire `<html>` element. This inverts every pixel of the Google Sheets UI (title bar, menus, toolbar, name box, formula bar, share/upgrade buttons, row/column headers, scrollbars, etc.) to a dark appearance in one shot. > - Then re-invert specific regions so they visually return to their original bright appearance: >   - `#waffle-grid-container` and `.grid-container` — so the spreadsheet cell grid area stays bright (the user explicitly wants the sheet content area to remain in light mode). >   - `img`, `video`, `iframe` — so icons/logos/thumbnails render with their original colors. >   - The toggle button itself (`#dark-mode-toggle-btn`) — so it displays with its intended blue/yellow colors. >   - Each of these uses `filter: invert(1) hue-rotate(180deg);` to cancel out the outer inversion. > - Both the outer and inner inversions must use `invert(1)` (not `0.92` or other values) so they perfectly cancel and the preserved regions look identical to the non-dark state. > > **Click behavior:** > - Clicking the button toggles the `dm-on` class on `<html>` and updates the button label between "🌙 Dark Mode" and "☀️ Light Mode". > > **Verification:** > - After injecting, programmatically click the button to enable dark mode, then take a screenshot to confirm: (1) the button is in the correct location and not covering the menu bar; (2) the entire chrome/UI (header, toolbar, formula bar, right-side share/upgrade buttons, row/column headers) appears dark; (3) the cell grid area remains bright; (4) icons and the button itself retain their original colors. > - If anything looks wrong, iterate until all four conditions are met. > > Implement this by executing JavaScript in the page. Do not rely on browser extensions or settings changes.
googleSheetsDarkMode.title: Dark Mode

googleSheetsHighlightActiveRowColumn.prompt: Highlight the row and column containing the currently active cell with a subtle background color, and apply alternating row background colors across the sheet for readability.
googleSheetsHighlightActiveRowColumn.title: Highlight Active Row Column

hackerNewsCardGridLayout.prompt: On story list pages only (front page, /newest, /best, /show, /ask), change the list display to a card grid that fills each row based on viewport width. Do not modify comment pages, user pages, or submission forms.
hackerNewsCardGridLayout.title: Card Grid Layout

hackerNewsDebateCardView.prompt: On a Hacker News comment page (/item?id=...), add a "Debate View" button. When clicked, AI reads the entire comment thread and synthesizes the two strongest opposing stances, rendering them as two side-by-side cards in clearly contrasting colors that work in both light and dark themes. Each card must include supporting quotes pulled from actual comments, with author attribution. Support translation into major languages.
hackerNewsDebateCardView.title: Debate Card View

wattpadImmersiveReadingMode.prompt: Only on story reading pages (URL contains /story/ or chapter identifiers), convert the page to an immersive reading mode: remove headers, footers, sidebars, comments sections, and promotional modules. Display the novel text centered in a paper-like column with infinite scroll. Add a floating button to switch between at least 4 theme colors: paper, sepia, dark, high-contrast.
wattpadImmersiveReadingMode.title: Immersive Reading Mode

wattpadTranslateChapter.prompt: Only on story reading pages, add a "Translate" button whose colors adapt to the current theme (light/dark/sepia). Support major languages. When clicked, translate the current chapter inline with a toggle for original / translation / side-by-side view.
wattpadTranslateChapter.title: Translate Chapter

xRemoveAllAds.prompt: Remove all ads from the timeline and sidebars, including promoted/sponsored posts and partnership ads, by hiding their DOM containers.

xSimplifyRightColumn.prompt: Keep the left navigation bar and the central timeline. Hide the right column entirely (Trends, Who to follow, Premium prompts, Live on X, etc.). Do not touch the left navigation.

youtubeHideShortsEverywhere.prompt: Hide the Shorts entry in the left sidebar, the Shorts shelf on the home feed, and any Shorts items that appear inside search results or recommendation rails. Do not hide primary navigation or break core page functionality.

youtubeRemoveVideoAds.prompt: Remove all in-video ads, including skippable, non-skippable, and mid-roll ads, by hiding their DOM containers and, when possible, programmatically skipping them. Do not remove sponsored segments embedded inside the video itself.
youtubeRemoveVideoAds.title: Remove Video Ads

starterPrompts.openClaw1: What have you been working on? Catch me up.
starterPrompts.openClaw2: Explore my workspace and tell me what you can help with
starterPrompts.openClaw3: What's the one thing I should do today that would make the biggest impact?
starterPrompts.siderClaw1Prompt: Visit the pricing pages of Notion.so, Asana.com, and Monday.com. For each tool, extract the team plan monthly price per user, what's included, and any free tier limits. Then compare them side by side and recommend the best value option for a 10-person team, explaining your reasoning.
starterPrompts.siderClaw1Title: Compare SaaS tool pricing across Notion, Asana, and Monday.com.
starterPrompts.siderClaw2Prompt: Go to Google Scholar and search for "LLM-based autonomous agents". Filter for papers published since 2024, then find the 10 most cited results. For each paper, extract the title, authors, citation count, publication venue, and link. Organize the results by citation count from highest to lowest.
starterPrompts.siderClaw2Title: Find the most cited papers on LLM agents from Google Scholar.
starterPrompts.siderClaw3Prompt: Go to Hacker News (news.ycombinator.com), scan today's front page, pick the 5 most important AI-related stories, and give me a quick briefing on each — include the headline, a one-sentence summary, why it matters, and the source link.
starterPrompts.siderClaw3Title: Today's top AI stories from Hacker News

demos.title: Create a Virtual Xylophone
demos.value: Create a HTML-based xylophone with colorful keys within an {{ARTIFACT}}. Provide visual feedback for struck keys, and generate simple, realistic sounds using the Web Audio API based on frequency parameters. Include basic instructions.

demos.title: Create a Food-Eating Emoji Game
demos.value: Create a simple HTML-based game, similar to Catch the Candy, within an {{ARTIFACT}}. The player controls an emoji at the bottom of the screen, moving it rapidly left and right to catch and eat food items (e.g., 🍓, 🍔, 🍎) falling from above while avoiding bombs (💣).Each time the player catches a food emoji, their emoji will change to a more exaggerated positive expression (e.g., 🤩, 😆, 😋) to indicate success. If the player accidentally catches a bomb, the emoji will first show an explosion (💥) and then change to a dizzy expression (😵‍💫).
